skill.name: discord-automation
skill.description: Automate Discord tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): messages, channels, roles, webhooks, reactions. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Discord tasks including messaging, role management, webhooks, and reactions via Rube MCP integration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation file (SKILL.md) that provides guidance on using Rube MCP for Discord automation. All static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown formatting for tool names, the URL is a legitimate service endpoint, and there is no executable code or malicious content. The skill is safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Manual Discord management takes time and is prone to errors. This skill enables Claude to automate Discord operations including messaging, role management, webhooks, and reactions through Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: Send and edit messages in Discord channels via bot
Create and manage direct messages to Discord users
Create, assign, and delete roles in Discord servers
Create and manage webhooks for external integrations
List guilds, channels, and members for automation
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection to function
Bot must have appropriate Discord permissions (SEND_MESSAGES, MANAGE_ROLES, etc.)
Rate limits apply to prevent Discord API throttling
Cannot edit messages sent by other bots or users

use_cases.title: Community Manager Notifications
use_cases.description: Automate welcome messages, announcements, and moderation alerts in Discord servers

use_cases.title: DevOps Deployment Alerts
use_cases.description: Set up automated notifications for CI/CD pipelines, server status, and deployment events

use_cases.title: Bot Development Prototyping
use_cases.description: Quickly test and prototype Discord bot functionality before building a full bot

prompt_templates.title: Send a Welcome Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the discord-automation skill to send a welcome message to channel ID {channel_id} saying 'Welcome to our server!'
prompt_templates.scenario: When a new member joins the Discord server

prompt_templates.title: Create and Assign a Role
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the discord-automation skill to create a new role called 'Verified' in guild {guild_id} and assign it to user {user_id}

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Webhook for Alerts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the discord-automation skill to create a webhook in channel {channel_id} named 'DeployAlerts' and show me how to execute it
prompt_templates.scenario: When setting up external service notifications

prompt_templates.title: Manage Server Reactions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the discord-automation skill to list all users who reacted with '✅' to message {message_id} in channel {channel_id}
prompt_templates.scenario: When tracking event RSVPs or approvals

output_examples.output: The bot will send the message to the specified channel. Success response includes message ID for editing or referencing later.
The bot creates the role and returns the role ID. You can then use this ID to assign the role to users.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before starting a workflow
Verify connection status shows ACTIVE before running any Discord operations
Respect Discord rate limits and handle 429 responses with Retry-After headers
anti_patterns: Assuming the bot has permissions it does not have - always verify permission setup first
Ignoring rate limits which can lead to temporary API bans
Using user operations (discord toolkit) instead of bot operations (discordbot) for automation

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a service by Composio that provides Model Context Protocol access to various integrations including Discord. It acts as a bridge between Claude and Discord's API.

faq.question: Do I need API keys to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. Rube MCP does not require API keys. You only need to add the MCP server endpoint and complete Discord OAuth authentication.

faq.question: Can this skill send messages as me or as a bot?
faq.answer: The skill supports both. Use the discordbot toolkit for bot token operations or the discord toolkit for user OAuth operations. Bot operations are preferred for automation.

faq.question: What permissions does the bot need?
faq.answer: Permissions vary by operation: SEND_MESSAGES for messaging, MANAGE_ROLES for role operations, MANAGE_WEBHOOKS for webhook management, and MANAGE_MESSAGES for reaction cleanup.

faq.question: How do I get channel and user IDs?
faq.answer: Enable Developer Mode in Discord settings, then right-click on any channel, user, or message to copy its ID. IDs are 64-bit snowflake numbers.

faq.question: Is this safe to use in production?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill provides documentation for using official Discord APIs. However, always test workflows in a development server first and follow Discord's rate limit guidelines.
